title: ranunculus orthorhynchus var. platyphyllus
intro: Ranunculus orthorhynchus var. platyphyllus, also known as the lesser celandine or fig buttercup, is a perennial plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as treating skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, due to its active compounds like ranunculin and other flavonoids. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and fever.
